page: Amazon Web Services z1d.large is a high-frequency, memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8151 processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU operates at a high clock speed of 4.0 GHz and includes 24 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 75 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps, and there are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile characterized by average single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1647.98 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1287.0. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform in the average tier. However, due to the low core count of a single physical core, multi-core benchmarks are poor, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 1543.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 2165.19. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 10% tier at 14.13 GB/sec and 12.15 GB/sec respectively.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the z1d.large provides high-frequency dedicated compute resources and fast local NVMe storage, making it suitable for workloads that are sensitive to per-core licensing costs or require high single-threaded performance. However, the low resource density in terms of core count limits its efficiency for highly parallelized tasks. Appropriate workloads include relational databases with high per-core licensing fees, single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and basic development tasks that benefit from high clock speeds and local SSD storage rather than massive parallel execution.
description: Amazon Web Services z1d.large is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring a high-frequency 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon 8151 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. It includes a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1287.0, but poor multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth due to the single physical core constraint. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads sensitive to per-core licensing or those requiring high single-threaded clock speeds. It is best suited for relational databases, single-threaded applications, and lightweight text processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.large is an x86_64 instance featuring a 4.0 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 75 GB NVMe SSD, offering average single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services z1d.large is a high-frequency instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 75 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, single-core instance featuring dedicated compute, fast local NVMe storage, and memory-optimized capacity for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8151 processor running at a high clock speed of 4.0 GHz
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8.0 GB per-core ratio
Bundled 75 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Average single-core performance paired with limited multi-core scaling
Well-suited for per-core licensed databases and single-threaded applications
